name: Tooheys Forest
subtitle: South Brisbane · Brisbane City Council
description: Tooheys Forest is one of Brisbane's best-kept secrets — a sprawling bushland reserve in the city's south packed with sandstone boulders hidden beneath a dense canopy. Brisbane City Council has formally recognised bouldering in the area and recently installed a new staircase to the main sector, marking a significant step forward for climbing access in Southeast Queensland.

name: White Rock
subtitle: Redbank Plains · Ipswich
description: Set within White Rock Conservation Park just south of Ipswich, this is one of Southeast Queensland's most significant and underdocumented sandstone climbing areas. Problems span multiple sectors across a diverse range of grades and styles. ACC is actively working with Ipswich City Council to establish formal access and build a comprehensive guidebook for the area.

name: Plunketts
subtitle: Yarrabilba · Logan City Council
description: Plunketts is an emerging bouldering destination in Logan's rapidly growing Yarrabilba corridor, offering quality sandstone problems in an accessible suburban setting. The area has real potential to become a well-used local crag for the region's expanding population. ACC has prioritised Plunketts for access assessment and documentation as the next phase of our Logan engagement.

name: Cedar Creek
subtitle: Cedar Creek · North Brisbane
description: Cedar Creek sits in a rainforest setting north of Brisbane, combining a popular swimming and recreation area with a surprisingly strong granite bouldering scene. The crag offers a wide variety of styles and grades with genuine potential for a large problem set. ACC is in the early stages of access research and council engagement with Moreton Bay City Council.

name: Passchendaele
subtitle: Stanthorpe · Southern Downs
description: Passchendaele Conservation Park near Stanthorpe is one of Queensland's most exciting undiscovered bouldering areas — densely packed with granite boulders across a large footprint and enormous potential for a varied and extensive problem set. The cooler Southern Downs climate makes it a natural destination for the warmer months. ACC has identified Passchendaele as a high-priority future crag and is beginning engagement with Southern Downs Regional Council.
